Three API Surfaces

Contextual exposes three distinct API surfaces. Clarity unifies them.
Registry API
native-object.{tenant}
.my.contextual.io
GET /api/v1/{type}
POST /api/v1/{type}
PATCH /api/v1/{type}/{id}
Auth: Client credentials
Audience: contextual/no-api
Management API
api.prod-001.prod.
contextual.io
POST /p/native-object-registry/types
POST /p/native-object/flow
POST /p/native-object/agent
POST /p/services/services
POST /p/agents/agents/{id}/start
Auth: Browser JWT
Audience: contextual/api
Auth API
/p/auth/
client-grants
PUT /p/auth/client-grants/{id}
Manages OAuth scopes
Per-agent credentials

The 10-Step Deploy Chain

Every /se:solution-deploy executes these in order with checkpointing.
1
POST /p/native-object-registry/types→ Object types
2
POST /p/native-object/api-configuration→ Connections
3
POST /p/native-object/ai-route→ AI routes
4
POST /p/native-object/flow→ Flows + flows_cred:{}
5
POST /p/native-object/agent→ Agents cond:"", semver
6
POST /p/services/services→ Service
7
PATCH /p/services/services-dependencies/{id}→ Attach deps
8
PUT .../services-dependencies/{id}/all→ Sync versions
9
POST /p/services/services/{id}/releases→ Release
10
POST /p/agents/agents/{id}/start→ Start

14 Commands — Full Lifecycle

Discover
/se:create
/se:discover
/se:check
Build
/se:scaffold
/se:flow-build
/se:map
Deploy
/se:component
/se:solution-deploy

/se:brief
/se:update

/se:meeting
/se:solution-wizard — spans all phases

Flow Generation

Before: 31 nodes, one at a time
After: one command
$ /se:flow-build bp-hokies \
  ai-document-extraction
Generated: 31 nodes, 5 blocks
Validation: PASS
Deployed: v1
31
nodes in 1 command

Node Block Library

6 composable blocks that chain into any flow pattern.
event-entrycontextual-start → log-tap → [flow] → contextual-end
                   catch → log-tap(error) → contextual-end
state-transitionget-native-object → log-tap → switch → function(patch) → patch-native-object → log-tap
record-queryfunction(query) → log-tap → query-native-object → log-tap → switch(found?)
record-createfunction(body) → log-tap → create-native-object → log-tap
ai-callfunction(prompt) → log-tap → ai-generate → log-tap → function(parse)
http-entryhttp-in → function → http-response

The Self-Learn Loop

Agent runs command
↓
Appends observations to expertise.yaml
↓
/se:self-improve validates against live Jira + tenant
↓
Confirmed facts integrated into knowledge base
↓
Next agent run starts smarter
"After 2 weeks on a project, any SE joining gets fully primed in one session."

What We Proved

CRITICAL DISCOVERIES

Agent PUT destroys envVar secretsBH-5
Registry API ≠ Management proxySchema-only types
contextual-start cond must be ""Lint rejects JSONata
flows_cred: {} requiredEditor shows empty
HTTP URL: .service. subdomainNot .{tenant} directly
PUT .../all syncs service depsUpdate All Components
Records PATCH: RFC 6902 onlyNot JSON merge
"We documented what the platform docs don't cover."

ComponentCreateReadListUpdateDelete
Object Types✓✓✓✓✓

Frequently Asked Questions

Does this replace the Contextual UI?
No. Clarity automates what SEs do repeatedly. The UI is still used for visual flow editing, monitoring, and one-off tasks.
What if the AI makes a mistake?
Approval gates in Paperclip let you review before anything touches a live tenant. All writes are dev only — prod is always read-only.
How long to onboard a new SE?
3 commands to get the full agent team. Under 5 minutes. expertise.yaml carries all project context forward.
What does it cost?
Uses your existing Claude Enterprise subscription. Paperclip is open source. No additional licensing.
Can other teams use this pattern?
Yes. The architecture is portable — define commands, package as skills, assign to agents. Works for any domain.
What about security?
Agents have Jira guardrails (test project only). Prod tenants are read-only. No secrets in git. Tokens expire in 24h.

Try it yourself.

Clone. Install. Run.
